Bluetooth driver test functions for starting, stopping, and handling HCI passthrough and RF test modes.

# Purpose
This C source code file defines a set of functions for testing Bluetooth driver functionality. The functions include [`bt_driver_test_start`](<#bt_driver_test_start>), [`bt_driver_test_stop`](<#bt_driver_test_stop>), and [`bt_driver_test_selftest`](<#bt_driver_test_selftest>), which are used to initiate, terminate, and perform self-tests on the Bluetooth driver, respectively. The function [`bt_driver_test_enter_hci_passthrough`](<#bt_driver_test_enter_hci_passthrough>) allows the driver to enter HCI passthrough mode, while [`bt_driver_test_handle_hci_passthrough_character`](<#bt_driver_test_handle_hci_passthrough_character>) processes characters in this mode. The function [`bt_driver_test_enter_rf_test_mode`](<#bt_driver_test_enter_rf_test_mode>) enables RF test mode, and [`bt_driver_test_set_spoof_address`](<#bt_driver_test_set_spoof_address>) sets a spoofed Bluetooth device address. The function [`bt_driver_test_mfi_chip_selftest`](<#bt_driver_test_mfi_chip_selftest>) performs a self-test specific to MFi chips, and [`bt_driver_core_dump`](<#bt_driver_core_dump>) is used to perform a core dump of the Bluetooth driver. The file includes the header `bluetooth/bt_test.h`, which likely provides necessary declarations and dependencies for these functions.
# Imports and Dependencies

---
- `bluetooth/bt_test.h`
